name: kubevirt_api
explanation: The KubeVirt API `virt-api` pod exposes Prometheus metrics over HTTPS on port 443 at `/metrics` (per README and tests). The check inherits `OpenMetricsBaseCheckV2` and additionally calls the Kubernetes API to enumerate VirtualMachine / VirtualMachineInstance custom resources. Both authentications are auto-injected: the metrics endpoint is reachable in-cluster and TLS verification is typically disabled (self-signed virt-api cert), and the Kubernetes API client uses `load_incluster_config()` with the Agent's ServiceAccount token (per pattern O). Spec marks no field as strictly required, but the check assumes `kubevirt_api_metrics_endpoint` is set. The endpoint URL is canonical (`https://<virt-api-pod>:443/metrics`), so an OpenMetrics port scan against the autodiscovered virt-api pod resolves it.
notes: Beyond OpenMetrics scraping, the check needs RBAC (`kubevirt.io:view`) bound to the Agent's ServiceAccount to list VMs/VMIs. The credential itself (in-cluster token) is auto-injected, but the cluster admin must apply the ClusterRoleBinding documented in the README — that's an out-of-band manual step that auto-config cannot perform.
